text:
Tajao (タハオ, Tahao) is the Vice Boss of the Cha-R Family and Military Advisor to the private army of Prince Luzurus Hui Guo Rou. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 384 

Appearance

Tajao is a middle-aged man who is of medium build. He sports a curtain haircut and dyed hair, his natural dark color visible in the shorter hair on his nape and his mustache. He has round eyes, noticeable dark circles, and gaunt cheeks. 

Personality

Tajao is a calm individual. He maintained his composure while discussing dire situations for his family. However, he was visibly affected by the suggestion that Luini may be after his boss' life, so he continued the conversation in an even tone and smoked unperturbedly. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

At 22:30 on the seventh day of the voyage, Tajao walks down a secret passage connecting the Cha-R Family Hideout on Tier 5 to the top tiers with Ken'i Wang while discussing Morena's disappearance. He proclaims himself in favor of letting the Royal Army search their hideout if it means doing the same to the ones of the Heil-Ly Family. When the two exit the passage and into the Cha-R Family Office, Ken'i introduces Tajao to Phinks, Nobunaga, and Feitan and tells him about the teleporting assassin. After listening to Ken'i's account, he agrees to prepare countermeasures and enquires about the Phantom Troupe's objective. Phinks replies and volunteers to take care of the assassin, stating his expectation that the assassin is bound to return to the Cha-R Family's hideout to use the passage to Tier 1. Tajao wonders why he would do that since Morena should have one as well, to which Phinks responds that perhaps he intends to murder his boss. Tajao warns the three Spiders that an all-out war may be approaching from which they cannot escape, depending on where Morena is found, due to the code of conduct among the three mafia families. He then weighs the pros and cons of Nobunaga's offer to kill Morena directly. 

On the twelfth day, Phinks and Feitan follow Nobunaga to the Cha-R family hideout on Tier 5 because he has something he wants to check with the Xi-Yu and Cha-R before heading to the Heil-Ly hideout to crush them. Phinks and Feitan sit as Nobunaga asks Tajao and Ken'i about the Heil-Ly. Nobunaga asks to confirm if they had known the Heil-Ly for a long time, and after hearing Tajao and Ken'i say that the Heil-Ly family's new boss had just recently taken over, he concludes that it would be hard for them to predict what the Heil-Ly were thinking. Nobunaga asks them if they think the Heil-Ly were really on the ship, and Tajao and Ken'i exchange a look before Tajao responds to Nobunaga, asking him who the Heil-Ly on the ship could be, if not the Heil-Ly family that their family had a history with. Ken'i shares that the Heil-Ly had the official succession ceremony attended by Brocco and the other higher-ups, before asking Nobunaga why he was asking. Tajao and Ken'i listen as Nobunaga shares his theory that the current Heil-Ly were trying to destroy everything. Tajao is surprised by this and asks what Nobunaga means by everything. Nobunaga elaborates that he believes the Heil-Ly were targeting everyone aboard the ship, essentially the Kakin Empire itself, and were killing to get supernatural powers to do so. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 

Tajao has reservations and says that both the Heil-Ly and them sound crazy, because they came up with the idea. He explains that the origins of the families were twisted to begin with, which is why balance was of such great importance to them, and that they were proud to have supported the country from the shadows. He explains that a fight is a last resort used to keep things in check, and that they had never thought of civilians, their customers, as targets. As he expresses his understanding of how the current Heil-Ly differed on the inside from the Kakin Mafia, he gets up and opens the secret door in the Cha-R family hideout, tells Nobunaga, Phinks, and Feitan that the Cha-R and Xi-Yu would cooperate with them, and officially asks them to crush the Heil-Ly. 

Tajao leads them down the walkway with a flashlight. As they walk, Tajao tells Nobunaga that he would keep his sword for him when he heads back. He explains that despite the official I.D.s he had given them, the military would not ignore such an obvious weapon. Nobunaga kicks up a fuss, and Tajao tells him that it was up to him, but warns Nobunaga that Tier 2 has a lot of young, inexperienced soldiers who might shoot him. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 He turns a wheel to open the door at the end of the tunnel and tells them that things would begin to be a bit rough from then on. 

They step out into a long, makeshift staircase without railings in a big chamber, which Tajao explains is next to the hull of the Black Whale. Phinks comments on the smell, and Tajao explains that the chamber they were in is dripping with waste from the 200,000 passengers on board. He shares that the recycling and sewage treatment facilities are between Tiers 4 and 5, and that Tier 5 was below the sewers. Nobunaga asks if the Cha-R and Xi-Yu manage the plants. Tajao affirms this and remarks that some things are convenient when you are the one handling the trash. Nobunaga guesses that the actual work is left to subcontractors, and that underlings were the ones managing the subcontractors, who would not report every time the subcontractors changed. Tajao realizes Nobunaga's implication, and the spiders reason out that the Heil-Ly disguised themselves as waste treatment personnel and pretended to be the employment agency to lure a whole mass of prey to them. Tajao comments that being around them made him feel like a model citizen.
